name: Snakebite Data Story Poster
summary: Raising awareness about the link between climate and snakebite
overview: As the second project from my Information Design studio, I created this data story poster that captures the link between different climate conditions and countries with high rates of snakebite.

name: Open Forum
summary: An app for organizing meaningful and engaging discussions, increasing students' sense of belongingness on campus
overview: Semester-long group project for Human-Computer Interaction consisting of a full UX research and design process to create a mobile app solution to support belongingness on campus

name: Snakebite Response Poster
summary: An illustrated guide to snakebite prevention for Batswana of all ages
overview: A poster and wallet card set for preventing snakebite in Botswana. The first of two projects for my Information Design studio.

name: Cooper Co-op Review Platform @ Sandbox
summary: Helping Northeastern students make more informed co-op searching decisions
overview: Leading the design side of the creation of a Northeastern co-op review platform

name: Dissonant Harmony
summary: Using type and form to convey "dissonant harmony" in Alexander Calder's "Big Crinkly"
overview: Marketing material for an Alexandar Calder museum exhibition. Includes an A1 size poster, A5 size flyer, and Instagram posts.

name: ALI Hacks
summary: Professional yet original internal portal UI and sponsorship package for a Boston-based, student-led hackathon
overview: High-fi prototypes for the internal application portal of a Boston student-led hackathon and a sponsorship package to send out to potential sponsors.

name: Rockumentary
summary: A recital poster that balances notes of rock and nostalgia
overview: A poster for my friend's British-rock-themed senior recital at Berklee College of Music

name: Text Diagram
summary: Showing the relationship between different topics covered in a podcast episode through a visual typographic map
overview: This is a map of the information covered in a podcast episode from the podcast “99% Invisible”. This project, focusing on information design, was done in the studio section for Design Process and Practices. It is one of my favorites from pieces I’ve done at school as it was when I discovered my attachment to information design. Unlike most designs that take lots of drafting and sketching, this one came together rather naturally.  I loved figuring out how to fit together all the information, establishing the typographic hierarchy, and coming up with the simple  lines and circle “arrows” that guide the viewer from one point to the next.

name: My Favorite Things Research Dossier
summary: A research dossier on "my favorite thing"

name: Radien
summary: A ticket-tracking system for project management and ease in handoff of work between teams
overview: A six-week long UI/UX research and design group project for Radien, as part of the User-Experience Design course.

name: Basics of Typography
overview: As someone who starts many more projects than I complete, this 24-hour challenge was the perfect way to give myself practice in making and committing to creative decisions, which is where I most often get stuck in the design process. During this one day, I successfully gathered and categorized all the information, drafted, and brought to life this poster that you see below (along with getting lots of practice with the Pathfinder tool in Illustrator).

name: Design Philosophy
overview: "Design is not just what it looks like and feels like. Design is how it works." (Steve Jobs) is my design philosophy and the quote that these cut up, layered, and rotated words represent. This project was for my studio for Typographic Systems and was for sure one of the more difficult design projects I’ve done as I was challenged to rethink how I use and orient type to not just be the literal text, but to make the design itself.
